pr.title: Split Python requirements into separate files
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Split the repository's Python dependency pins into purpose-specific requirement sets under reqs/, updated contributor docs and GitHub package scripts to install the narrower requirement file they need, and began moving away from one monolithic requirements.in. The stated motivation was improving new contributor experience; the practical effect is clearer dependency ownership for decomp tooling, build tooling, diff tooling, dev formatting, misc scripts, and progress page generation.
what_changed: Added separate pip-compile input and pinned output files under reqs/: build, decomp, dev, diff, gen-pages, and misc.

Moved decomp-related dependencies into reqs/decomp.in and reqs/decomp.txt, including colorama, m2c from GitHub, pcpp, pyperclip, Pygments, and pyelftools, plus transitive pins such as graphviz and pycparser.
Moved formatter/development dependencies into reqs/dev.in and reqs/dev.txt, specifically black and isort with their transitive pins.
Moved diff helper dependencies into reqs/diff.in and reqs/diff.txt, preserving the TODO for asm-differ and pinning humanfriendly and argcomplete.
Added focused requirement sets for build tooling, progress page generation, and misc scripts: requests for build, pcpp for gen-pages, and GitPython for misc.
Deleted the old requirements.in file shown in the diff excerpt, which had mixed decomp, dev, diff, misc, and other dependencies in one list.
Updated .github/README.md contributor instructions to install reqs/decomp.txt instead of requirements.txt for decomp.py usage.
Updated gen-pages Docker/package scripts to copy and install reqs/gen-pages.txt instead of the monolithic requirements.txt.
Adjusted .github/packages/build-linux/Dockerfile so the gen-pages stage copies /reqs/gen-pages.txt into /tmp/reqs/ before setup.sh runs.
smart_moves: Grouped Python dependencies by workflow rather than asking every contributor or container to install all tooling dependencies.
Kept both .in source files and pip-compile-generated .txt lock files for each dependency group, preserving the existing reproducible pinning workflow while making dependency ownership clearer.
Updated the contributor-facing README at the same time as the requirement split, avoiding stale setup instructions for the main decomp workflow.
Updated Docker/setup entrypoints to use the minimal gen-pages dependency set, reducing container setup scope and avoiding accidental reliance on unrelated repo-wide Python packages.
Named requirement groups after concrete use cases, such as decomp, diff, dev, build, gen-pages, and misc, which makes future install commands and maintenance easier to search and reason about.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation contributor onboarding, prefer a small task-specific install target such as reqs/decomp.txt over a repository-wide requirements.txt when only decomp.py/m2c-related tooling is needed.
When splitting dependency files, update all consumers in the same PR: docs, Dockerfiles, setup scripts, entrypoints, and any CI/package scripts that install Python requirements.
Maintain pip-compile provenance comments in generated .txt files so future maintainers know which .in file generated each lock file.
Use separate requirement files to make toolchain failures easier to isolate: a gen-pages dependency issue should not be mixed with decomp.py, formatter, or asm-differ helper dependencies.
Dependency organization can be part of decomp project ergonomics even without touching C matching code; clean setup paths reduce friction for new contributors.
naming_conventions: Purpose names in this PR are short workflow labels: build, decomp, dev, diff, gen-pages, and misc.
Generated requirement files retain pip-compile header comments naming the exact input file, for example pip-compile reqs/decomp.in.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactics were changed in this PR.
No function-level decompilation, symbol naming, or C/ASM matching evidence appears in the provided PR slice.
The only matching-adjacent detail is preserving decomp tooling dependencies such as m2c and pyelftools in reqs/decomp.txt.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body says this was part of a mission to improve new contributor experience and would be explained in documentation later.
follow_up_queries: Find later documentation PRs explaining the new reqs/ dependency groups for contributors.
Search for remaining installs of requirements.txt to see whether all workflows migrated to purpose-specific reqs/*.txt files.
Check whether requirements.txt remained as a compatibility aggregate after requirements.in was deleted, or whether it was removed in a later cleanup.
Search for later updates to reqs/diff.in involving asm-differ editable installs, since this PR preserved a TODO for that dependency.
